frozen_components: reference model θ_ref (used only for KL regularization, not for gradients)
architecture.summary: LaTRO formulates reasoning as sampling from a latent distribution and optimizes it via a variational framework. A single LLM θ jointly learns to generate reasoning rationales (the 'reasoner') and to evaluate their quality via self-generated reward signals (the 'reward function'). The model conditions on a query, samples multiple reasoning trajectories internally, estimates their value by the log-likelihood of the correct answer following that trajectory, and updates parameters to favor high-quality rationales while maintaining KL regularization to a frozen reference model θ_ref. Information flow: query → sample reasoning rationale → compute likelihood of correct answer given rationale → gradient update on both rationale generation and answer prediction.
trained_components: self-reward evaluator (log-likelihood of correct answer, within θ)
answer predictor conditioned on rationale (within θ)

biblio.title: Language Models are Hidden Reasoners: Unlocking Latent Reasoning Capabilities via Self-Rewarding

claims.statement: On GSM8K, LaTRO improves zero-shot accuracy by an average of 12.5% over base models across Phi-3.5-mini, Mistral-7B, and Llama-3.1-8B with greedy decoding
On GSM8K, LaTRO improves zero-shot accuracy by an average of 9.6% over supervised fine-tuning across Phi-3.5-mini, Mistral-7B, and Llama-3.1-8B with greedy decoding
On GSM8K with self-consistency (maj@8), LaTRO achieves 90.5% accuracy for Phi-3.5, 73.8% for Mistral-7B, and 87.0% for Llama-3.1-8B
On ARC-Challenge with greedy decoding, LaTRO improves over base models by up to 2.9% with an average increase of 1.9%
LaTRO enables LLMs to concurrently improve both their reasoning process and ability to evaluate reasoning quality, without requiring external feedback or reward models

LaTRO can train LLMs to generate more concise rationales; when LaTRO is trained with maximum token length of 200, it significantly improves performance under this constraint compared to models trained with longer limits
LaTRO equipped with self-consistency can further enhance performance even with LaTRO's focus on training-time optimization; inference-time scaling remains beneficial

For ARC-Challenge, LaTRO shows smaller improvements compared to GSM8K; the models are already good at producing the answer either directly or through CoT prompting, hence further optimization of the reasoning process did not yield significant improvement
